summary: Crisp audience tape of a mellow mountain‑side set that shines on Watchtower and a strong China > Rider, with solid sound despite occasional crowd noise.
review: The recording captures an outdoor, laid‑back show with clear, wide‑spectrum audience sound; listeners repeatedly point out the superb Watchtower performance and a standout China > Rider. While most fans praise the vibe and Jerry’s guitar work, a few note weak moments such as the Big Boss Man opener and a lackluster Push, giving the concert an overall mixed but enjoyable feel.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 3‑4 stars and highlight several strong songs; recording quality is consistently described as good to excellent, yielding a weighted overall rating around 3.7‑3.9.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Super‑crisp, wide‑spectrum audience tape with occasional intrusive crowd noise and a slight ‘hollow’ outdoor ambience; overall clear mix with good instrument separation.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A mellow, mountain‑side vibe that builds momentum after the first set; highlights emerge in the second half with strong transitions and memorable solos.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for ripping guitar riffs on Watchtower, Loser and Stella Blue; described as “the man” by several reviewers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as present from tuning through the Zeppelin‑style riff into China Cat; no negative comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brief mention of his shorts during a chilly night; otherwise not specifically commented on.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) highlighted for twinkling work on Cassidy and occasional supportive parts; no criticism.
band_member_comments.Drums: One reviewer called the drum segment “sinister” but overall drumming was seen as adequate.
